title: Doronicum tussilaginis
intro: Doronicum tussilaginis, also known as Golden Hawksbeard, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Doronicum tussilaginis include flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
